title: Emergent Dispersive Multipolar Excitations in NaErSe$_{2}$
authors: Zheng Zhang, Mingfang Shu, Mingtai Xie, Weizhen Zhuo, Yanzhen Cai, Christian Balz, Jianting Ji, Feng Jin, Jie Ma
abstract: In most condensed-matter systems, local and collective excitations remain decoupled due to their distinct energy scales. Here, we identify coupled local-collective excitations in the triangular antiferromagnet NaErSe$_2$ by combining neutron spectroscopy with total angular momentum modeling. The low-lying crystalline electric field (CEF) doublets include a dipolar $Γ_4$ ground state forming stripe-$x$ order and a $Γ_{5,6}$ excited state with dipole-octupole character. High-resolution spectra reveal emergent symmetry-selected dispersions, where magnon branches from the ground state are replicated on higher $Γ_4$ levels but couple with the $Γ_{5,6}$ levels to form a distinct multipolar band. An applied magnetic field reconstructs the CEF wavefunctions and polarizes the system into a multipolar ferromagnet, further reshaping the spectra. This study demonstrates the emergent coupling of local and collective excitations driven by strong spin-orbit coupling and establishes NaErSe$_2$ as a platform for field-tunable multipolar excitations in frustrated magnets.
summary: This paper demonstrates how the interaction between local crystal field levels and collective magnons in NaErSe2 gives rise to new, dispersive multipolar excitations. It shows that these excitations can be tuned using an external magnetic field to create a multipolar ferromagnetic state. This provides a new platform for studying complex, high-rank multipolar physics in frustrated magnets.
main_problem: The study aims to understand the coupling between local crystalline electric field (CEF) excitations and collective spin dynamics, specifically how this coupling leads to emergent dispersive multipolar excitations.
main_result: The researchers identified that the coupling between ground-state magnons and higher-lying dipole-octupole CEF states creates a distinct, symmetry-selected multipolar band, and demonstrated that magnetic fields can induce a transition to a multipolar ferromagnet.
method: The work combines inelastic neutron scattering (INS) and Raman spectroscopy with theoretical modeling using the J = 15/2 manifold approach, Linear Spin Wave theory, and SU(N) coherent state simulations.
model_or_system: The system is NaErSe2, a triangular-lattice antiferromagnet containing Er3+ ions with a 4f11 configuration. The model uses a total angular momentum J = 15/2 Hamiltonian including both CEF terms and anisotropic exchange interactions.
key_observables: Inelastic neutron scattering spectra, magnetic Bragg peaks, CEF energy levels, and magnetization/specific heat measurements.
important_parameters: CEF splittings (e.g., 1.2, 3.8, 5.3, and 6.0 meV), exchange parameters (Jzz, Jplus, Jplusplus, Jzplus), and magnetic field strengths up to 7.5 T.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the J = 15/2 manifold approach and uses the D3d symmetry of the Er3+ environment to classify the CEF levels.
figures_summary: The figures likely display neutron scattering intensity maps (constant-energy cuts), spin-wave dispersions, CEF energy levels, and the evolution of the spectra under applied magnetic fields.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of local-collective coupling, describes the experimental setup and material properties, presents the identification of CEF levels and magnetic order, details the discovery of dispersive multipolar modes, and concludes with the effects of high magnetic fields.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Fig. 2. Stripe-x magnetic order and spin excitations in NaErSe2. (a) Elastic neutron scattering intensity map at 50 mK, showing a magnetic Bragg peak at the M point. A corresponding intensity cut is shown in (d). (b) Reciprocal space diagram illustrating the high-symmetry points and measurement path. (c) The determined stripe-x magnetic structure. (e) Measured low-energy spin-wave spectrum along the (H, H, 1.5) direction. This is compared with calculations using an effective spin-1/2 LSW model (f) and a J = 15/2 SU(N) model (h) . The inset in (f) shows the three degenerate stripe configurations. (g) Constant- ⃗Q energy cuts at the M and Γ points, comparing experimental data with both...

Fig. 3. Dispersive CEF excitations in NaErSe2. (a, c, e) Experi- mental INS spectra measured at T = 50 mK with incident energies Ei = 1.77, 3.7, and 12.14 meV, respectively. (b, d, f) Correspond- ing theoretical calculations using the J = 15/2 SU(N) model. The momentum path is along (H, H, 0) for (a, b) and along (H, H, 1.5) for (c-f). (g, h) High-resolution SU(N) calculations showing the de- tailed dispersions of the ground state and the first four CEF excita- tions, labeled with their respective symmetry (e.g., Γ4, Γ5,6).
Fig. 4. INS spectra under magnetic fields of 4.0 T and 7.5 T. Panels (a, c) show experimental data along the (H, H, 1.5) direction at 50 mK, while (b, d) are the corresponding SU(N) calculations.
